claim_status: HTTPS host-alias harness plan only; no browser run, no Servo adoption, no Turing compatibility claim, no Chrome-class claim, no WPT/Test262 pass-rate claim, no HTTPS execution evidence, and no release-code authorization.

tls_plan.certificate_strategy: future ephemeral local test certificate or local test CA generated outside source control for each retained run
tls_plan.tls_version_min: TLS 1.2 until TLS 1.3-only policy is reviewed for Servo and comparison browsers
tls_plan.trust_store_modification_policy: future browser runs must use an isolated profile trust store or a captured temporary OS trust change with before/after proof and cleanup; persistent trust-store changes are not allowed by this plan
certificate_artifacts_required: certificate subject, issuer, SAN list, validity window, serial, fingerprint, and generation command
private-key non-commitment proof and artifact-location policy
trust-store or isolated-profile trust change before/after record
TLS protocol, cipher, SNI, certificate verification, and handshake failure logs
cleanup proof for every generated certificate, trust entry, and temporary profile

host_alias_plan.mode: reviewed transient host alias required for browser execution; HTTP Host headers are acceptable only for the no-browser route self-test
required_artifacts: host-to-loopback mapping method, scope, timestamp, and owner
before/after resolver or hosts-file state when OS state is touched
browser-visible origin and address proof for every turing.invalid host
cleanup proof showing no persistent host alias, resolver, proxy, certificate, or profile mutation remains
failure record for every mapping, cleanup, permission, elevation, or resolver-cache error

required_browser_run_record: source baseline, Servo binary path, binary hash, build profile, feature flags, JavaScript runtime/JIT state, and selected ADR-0009 option
operating system, hardware, GPU, driver, display, power, thermal, sandbox, process-mode, profile, cache, and temporary directory metadata
HTTPS server command, bound port, certificate fingerprint, SAN list, trust-store state, host-alias state, SNI proof, and cleanup proof
per-origin route result, HTTP status, TLS state, console log, blocked-reason log, screenshot or semantic trace, and SHA-256 artifact hash
per-case pass, fail, timeout, crash, error, skip, expected-failure, unexpected-pass, subtest-failure, disabled-test, unsupported-API, flake, rerun, and failure-denominator accounting
statement of unsupported behavior and a no-claim result boundary approved before comparison or publication
prohibited_claims: performance, memory, energy, accessibility, security, daily-driver, beta, stable, production, or release readiness
unsupported: no HTTPS server has been executed by this plan
no certificate, private key, host alias, trust-store entry, or browser profile is generated by this plan
no Servo, Turing, or competitor browser is launched by this plan
no WPT or Test262 command is run by this plan
no compatibility, security, performance, accessibility, Chrome-class, release, or production claim is allowed
